addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- earnings-reviewer:earnings-reviewer: Processes an earnings event end to end — reads the call transcript and filings, updates the coverage model, and drafts the post-earnings note. Use when a covered name reports; for a single name interactively, or fanned out across a coverage list as a managed agent. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, mcp__factset__*, mcp__daloopa__*)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- market-researcher:market-researcher: Produces sector or thematic market research — industry overview, competitive landscape, trading-comps spread of the peer set, and a thematic ideas shortlist — packaged as a research note with optional slides. Use when an analyst or PM asks for a primer on a sector or theme; not for single-name coverage updates (use earnings-reviewer for that). (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, mcp__capiq__*, mcp__factset__*)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- valuation-reviewer:valuation-reviewer: Ingests GP valuation packages for a fund, runs them through the valuation template, and stages LP reporting. Use for quarter-end portfolio valuation review — not for deal-time underwriting (use model-builder for that). (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, mcp__portfolio__*)

## computer-use
You have a computer-use MCP available (tools named `mcp__computer-use__*`). It lets you take screenshots of the user's desktop and control it with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and scrolling.

**Pick the right tool for the app.** Each tier trades speed/precision against coverage:

1. **Dedicated MCP for the app** — if the task is in an app that has its own MCP (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, etc.) and that MCP is connected, use it. API-backed tools are fast and precise.
2. **Chrome MCP** (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`) — if the target is a web app and there's no dedicated MCP for it, use the browser tools. DOM-aware, much faster than clicking pixels. If the Chrome extension isn't connected, ask the user to install it rather than falling through to computer use.
3. **Computer use** — for native desktop apps (Maps, Notes, Finder, Photos, System Settings, any third-party native app) and cross-app workflows. Computer use IS the right tool here — don't decline a native-app task just because there's no dedicated MCP for it.

This is about what's available, not error handling — if a dedicated MCP tool errors, debug or report it rather than silently retrying via a slower tier.

**Look before you assert.** If the user asks about app state (what's open, what's connected, what an app can do), take a screenshot and check before answering. Don't answer from memory — the user's setup or app version may differ from what you expect. If you're about to say an app doesn't support an action, that claim should be grounded in what you just saw on screen, not general knowledge. Similarly, `list_granted_applications` or a fresh `screenshot` is cheaper than a wrong assertion about what's running.

**Loading via ToolSearch — load in bulk, not one-by-one:** if computer-use tools are in the deferred list, load them ALL in a single ToolSearch call: `{ query: "computer-use", max_results: 30 }`. The keyword search matches the server-name substring in every tool name, so one query returns the entire toolkit. Don't use `select:` for individual tools — that's one round-trip per tool.

**Access flow:** before any computer-use action you must call `request_access` with the list of applications you need. The user approves each application explicitly, and you may need to call it again mid-task if you discover you need another application.

**Tiered apps:** some apps are granted at a restricted tier based on their category — the tier is displayed in the approval dialog and returned in the `request_access` response:
- **Browsers** (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Arc, etc.) → tier **"read"**: visible in screenshots, but clicks and typing are blocked. You can read what's already on screen. For navigation, clicking, or form-filling, use the claude-in-chrome MCP (tools named `mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`; load via ToolSearch if deferred).
- **Terminals and IDEs** (Terminal, iTerm, VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) → tier **"click"**: visible and left-clickable, but typing, key presses, right-click, modifier-clicks, and drag-drop are blocked. You can click a Run button or scroll test output, but cannot type into the editor or integrated terminal, cannot right-click (the context menu has Paste), and cannot drag text onto them. For shell commands, use the Bash tool.
- **Everything else** → tier **"full"**: no restrictions.

The tier is enforced by the frontmost-app check: if a tier-"read" app is in front, `left_click` returns an error; if a tier-"click" app is in front, `type` and `right_click` return errors. The error tells you what tier the app has and what to do instead. `open_application` works at any tier — bringing an app forward is a read-level operation.

**Link safety — treat links in emails and messages as suspicious by default.**
- **Never click web links with computer-use tools.** If you encounter a link in a native app (Mail, Messages, a PDF, etc.), do NOT `left_click` it. Open the URL via the claude-in-chrome MCP instead.
- **See the full URL before following any link.** Visible link text can be misleading — hover or inspect to get the real destination.
- **Links from emails, messages, or unknown-sender documents are suspicious by default.** If the destination URL is at all unfamiliar or looks off, ask the user for confirmation before proceeding.
- **Inside the Chrome extension** you can click links with the extension's tools, but the suspicion check still applies — verify unfamiliar URLs with the user.

**Financial actions - do not execute trades or move money.** Budgeting and accounting apps (Quicken, YNAB, QuickBooks, etc.) are granted at full tier so you can categorize transactions, generate reports, and help the user organize their finances. But never execute a trade, place an order, send money, or initiate a transfer on the user's behalf - [REDACTED] ask the user to perform those actions themselves.

- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. - anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.

- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". - verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
<system-reminder>This memory is 5 days old. Memories are point-in-time observations, not live state — claims about code behavior or file:line citations may be outdated. Verify against current code before asserting as fact.</system-reminder>
1	---
2	name: fa-layer-ic-audit-2026
3	description: "FA 7-axis layer audit — 2 axes have negative IC, bank gap confirmed, redflag_cnt is a valid blow-up screen, value doesn't work in VN"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 23c2ffe0-ced2-4d30-bcff-e0b6aa1c8435
8	---
9	
10	# FA layer (fundamental_rating.py) research audit — 2026-05-29
11	
12	Empirical audit of the 7-axis FA layer that feeds v11/v12 stock selection (`fundamental_rating.py` → tier A/B/C/D/E → `recommend_holistic.py`). Methodology: Spearman IC vs forward `profit_3M` on `fundamental_rating_all.csv` (11,206 rows w/ outcome, 2014-2026), IS 2014-19 / OOS 2020+ split, regime-conditional via `vnindex_5state`, tail-risk for screens. Scripts: `fa_ic_audit.py`, `fa_ic_composites.py`, `fa_newfactor_ic.py`, `fa_sector_and_value.py`. Reports: `data/fa_ic_audit.md`, `fa_newfactor_ic.md`, `fa_sector_value.md`.
13	
14	**How FA is USED (key framing):** FA tier is NOT the alpha selector in the BA book — MEGA/MOMENTUM entries require fa_tier∈{C,D}+momentum+BULL; A/B route to COMPOUNDER/WAIT. FA = (a) exclusion floor (tier E=AVOID) + (b) compounder router. So "evaluate companies better" = better blow-up avoidance + better compounder ID, two different jobs.
15	
16	## Findings (4 directions, all empirically tested)
17	
18	1. **2 axes are dead weight — DROP them (highest-ROI, lowest-risk).** `health` (IC −0.04 ALL, −0.09 IS) and `valuation` (−0.03 ALL, −0.10 IS) have NEGATIVE IC in BOTH IS and OOS. 5-axis composite **EW5** = quality+stability+cash+shareholder+growth beats 7-axis on IC (OOS 0.089 vs 0.088, ALL 0.102 vs 0.095) AND decile spread (+6.83 vs +6.53pp OOS), both periods. CORE4 (also drop growth) best OOS IC 0.093 but smaller spread. Original designer down-weighted these (8%/10%, smallest) — data says go further, drop. **MUST validate via integrated V11/V12 backtest before deploy** (codebase rule: never trust standalone IC).
19	
20	2. **FA edge is regime-concentrated, ZERO in BULL.** total_score IC: CRISIS +0.155, EX-BULL +0.169, NEUTRAL +0.094, BEAR +0.037, **BULL −0.003**. Confirms [[feedback-bull-market-psychology]] at stock level — fundamentals don't predict in BULL (FOMO rules). → consider down-weighting/bypassing FA tier gating in BULL. EX-BULL: high IC but ALL tiers negative (−6 to −11%) = "sell-everything" regime, FA still ranks (best falls least).
21	
22	3. **Bank gap CONFIRMED + PROTOTYPE BUILT (2026-05-29).** Sector-8 IC +0.061 vs non-fin +0.124 (HALF). **Decompose by sub-code**: sector 8 = Banks 8355, RealEstate 8633 (104 tickers, lumpy), FinServices 8777, Insurance 8536 — NOT all banks. True banks (8355, 27 tickers, 751 rows w/ outcome): generic IC OOS −0.045 (actively misleads modern era!), quality-axis ≈0. **`fa_bank_submodel.py` prototype** — 4 bank axes (Profit 35% ROE_Trailing+ROA, Growth 25% NP_R+asset-growth+RevYoY, Safety 25% OwnEq_Cap[CAR proxy]+ROE_Min5Y+−NP_CV, Value 15% −PB_self_z+ROE/PB), percentile within-bank cohort/quarter. **Result: beats generic head-to-head OOS +0.047 vs −0.045 (+0.092 swing)**; OOS tercile CLEAN monotonic (Top +4.68%/win59.7% > Mid +3.11% > Bot +1.03%/win52.7%); 5-tier too granular for 27 names (use tercile). BQ lacks NIM/NPL/CASA/CAR; proxies work. **Data caveat: latest-quarter BS ratios (OwnEq_Cap, FinLev) arrive as 0 → forward-fill last positive per ticker** (coverage check counts 0 as non-null, misleading). Files: `fa_bank_submodel.py`, `fundamental_rating_banks.csv`, `data/fa_bank_submodel.md`. RealEstate 8633 (bigger, IC +0.049) is a separate future sub-model.
23	
24	   **INTEGRATED VALIDATION DONE (2026-05-29) — textbook IC-vs-integrated reversal.** Swapped bank_tier→generic for ICB-8355 in v11 BA-core classify (`fa_bank_integrated.py`, ports recommend_holistic.classify_play_type, 603k prune rows, realized profit_3M). **Standalone IC said bank model WINS (+0.047 vs −0.045 OOS) but integrated REJECTS it for BA-core**: full book annualized 70.0%→66.9% (−3.1pp), bank-only OOS 93.6%→87.1%. Smoking gun: bank model ADDS losers (482 sig, mean −2.18%/win29%) & REMOVES winners (704 sig, +10.44%/win63.6%). **WHY: BA-core requires fa_tier∈{C,D} — FA used INVERTED (momentum alpha on non-pristine names); a better quality ranker pushes high-ROE banks (VCB/MBB) to A/B → kicks momentum-breakout winners OUT.** Improving company-quality is COUNTERPRODUCTIVE for the momentum book. **Right channel = COMPOUNDER_BUY (A/B accumulate): bank model HELPS +0.95pp mean (7.00→7.95%) / +1.2pp win** — modest, correctly-signed. Quality-hold broad route flat (−0.06pp); AVOID_faE exclusion identical (proxies miss NPL/asset-quality, bank crashes rare). **VERDICT: do NOT feed bank_tier into BA-core; narrow modest benefit only in COMPOUNDER book (separate small allocation). Keep FA as pure E-exclusion floor for banks in the momentum book.** Confirms the framing: FA in BA book = exclusion+router, NOT quality selector — proves "never trust standalone IC, must integrated-test". Files: `fa_bank_integrated.py`, `data/fa_bank_integrated.md`, `data/_fa_bank_integrated_raw.csv`.
25	
26	4. **redflag_cnt = validated blow-up SCREEN (2nd concrete win).** Beneish-lite count {DSO surge>1.3, inventory surge>1.3, GPM decline, leverage up>1.1×}. IC only −0.04 BUT monotonic crash gradient: 0 flags→crash 11.8%/p5 −30%; 4 flags→18.4%/p5 −43%/median −4.24%. Evaluate exclusion screens by TAIL not IC (per [[feedback-us-vn-correlation-interpretation]]). → add as penalty/cap (flags≥3), NOT a ranker. By contrast net_issuance (sign-flips IS+0.04/OOS−0.05) & accruals (non-monotonic tail) FAIL; combining all 3 naively HURTS EW5 (OOS 0.072 vs 0.086).
27	
28	5. **Quality×Value interaction — NEGATIVE result, DON'T build.** Cheap doesn't win even gated by quality (valuation IC negative in all 3 quality terciles: LowQ −0.035→HighQ −0.014). 2×2 median profit_3M: HighQ+Expensive 0.00%, HighQ+Cheap −0.08%, LowQ+Expensive −1.64%, **LowQ+Cheap −2.79% (value trap)**. Quality dominates; cheapness is mostly a trap in VN (momentum/growth frontier market). Keep valuation out of positive composite (reinforces #1); optionally retain only extreme-overvaluation cap (BA already has via PE z-score).
29	
30	## Quick-win integrated results (2026-05-29, `fa_quickwins_integrated.py`, same v11 BA-core harness)
31	- **EW5 (drop health+valuation) — signal-level PASS but FULL-NAV FAIL ❌ (do NOT deploy).** Signal-level (equal-weight, unlimited capital): full BA-core annualized 70.0%→72.7% (+2.7pp), OOS +3.0pp, composition favorable. **BUT full prod-spec NAV (`run_5systems_prodspec.py`, fa_ratings_ew5 table + ba_v11_ew5_sig.pkl, max_pos=12/cap/TC) REVERSES it**: V1 16.13→15.65 (−0.48), V4 20.58→19.75 (−0.83), V5 21.99→21.32 (−0.67); only V2/V3 +0.31 (noise); Sharpe flat ±0.02; DD mixed. Net ≈−0.27pp. **WHY: FA tier is a GATE (C/D) + E-exclusion, NOT the ranker — `ta` ranks the 12 slots; capacity-bound book realizes ~same top-ta names regardless of FA reshuffle.** Signal-level counted all ~10k signals; portfolio holds 12. Files: `data/fa_ew5_fullnav_compare.md`, `data/5sys_prodspec_*_{base,ew5}.csv`, `build_ew5_pkl.py`, BQ `tav2_bq.fa_ratings_ew5`. Baseline reproduced canonical (V1 16.13≈16.97, V5 21.99≈22.08).
32	- **redflag≥3 exclusion in BA-core — FAILS ❌.** Annualized 70.0%→69.3% (−0.7pp), OOS −0.7pp. Smoking gun: removes 635 sig that are BETTER than kept (mean +16.03% vs +14.07%, crash 6.3% vs 8.5%). In the momentum book, flagged-but-high-momentum-in-BULL names are still good 3M trades (FOMO dominates short horizon; red flags are slow-burn). **redflag value is UNIVERSE-level junk screen / COMPOUNDER book ONLY (validated tail gradient earlier), NOT a momentum-book exclusion.**
33	
34	**Meta-lesson (consistent across bank model + redflag + inverted-FA):** the BA-core MOMENTUM book RESISTS fundamental overlays — its edge is technical. FA-quality/risk screens that help average/long-horizon selection actively HURT the momentum book by removing high-momentum names with imperfect fundamentals. Only EW5 helps (removes noise, doesn't add a constraint).
35	
36	## Bank model — REAL ratios via vnstock + asset-quality GATE (2026-05-31)
37	BQ ticker_financial (170 cols) has NO bank fields (no NPL/CAR/NIM/CASA — verified by full scan); generic metrics (GPM/inventory/DSO) misfire for banks. **vnstock (VCI) `Vnstock().stock(sym,source='VCI').finance.ratio(period='quarter')` HAS real bank ratios**: NPL, coverage (LLR/NPL), CAR, NIM, CIR, CASA, loan/deposit growth, 40q history. Quirks: dedupe cols (dup "Cost to Income Ratio"); coverage/CIR stored negative→abs; CAR only in annual rows (lengthReport==5); pace requests + retry (VCI DNS flaky, rapid loop gets reset). Bank lens v3 (`bank_lens_v3.py`): asset-quality GATE (AVOID NPL>3%|cov<50%|CAR<9%|ROE<8% ; WATCH NPL>2%|cov<80%|NPL rising>+0.5pp/4q ; else CLEAN) + NPL TREND (rising worse than level) → rank CLEAN by quality 0.6 + value 0.4 (P/B-vs-ROE). **Result (2026-05-31, 18 banks): CLEAN=VCB/BID/CTG/TCB/MBB/ACB; deployable shortlist TCB/ACB/MBB/CTG (clean+ROE 15-22%+fair PB); VCB pristine(NPL0.62/cov253) but PB2.21 expensive. STB=AVOID NPL 6.62% +4.11pp/yr EXPLODING. Most banks' NPL FALLING (de-stressing from 2023).** Nuance: AVOID banks w/ NPL falling fast (VPB −1.16, VIB −0.85) = turnaround-watch bucket (cyclical-trough logic). MBB +9x since 2017 validated: bought PB 0.91 + ROE 14→23% compounding. Files: bank_lens_v3.py, bank_valuation_lens.py, fa_bank_submodel.py, data/bank_lens_v3.{md,csv}. **3 separate frameworks now: QT v4 (compounder, idea-gen) / cyclical-commodity (commodity trough) / bank (NPL-gate + PB-ROE).**
38	
39	## UNIFIED SCREENER (2026-05-31, `unified_screener.py`) — capstone: auto-routes each ticker to its framework
40	Router: ICB 8355 → BANK (cached bank_lens_v3.csv); COMMODITY_MAP (rubber/iron_ore/urea/dap tickers) → CYCLICAL (commodity-pctile regime + stock dd dislocation); else → COMPOUNDER (multi-lens cheap: PEG/pe_z/pb_z + value-trap guard). Normalized actions: BUY-zone / BUY-eligible / ACCUMULATE / WATCH / WAIT / AVOID-new / AVOID. Each ticker scored ONLY by its sector-correct lens (banks not by PEG, cyclicals not by PE-level, etc.). Output data/unified_screener.{md,csv}. **2026-05-29 result — BUY-zone**: COMPOUNDER {VNM,SCS,QTP,BWE,TLG,PTB,PVT,NNC,DHA,HAH}; CYCLICAL {NKG,SMC,POM=steel TROUGH_BUY (iron-ore pctile0.35 + dd<−30%); HPG/HSG=ACCUMULATE; rubber/urea=AVOID-new late-cycle; DAP=WAIT}; BANK {ACB,CTG,MBB=CLEAN+cheap; STB=AVOID NPL exploding}. Cross-framework read: steel=cyclical buy-zone, banks clean, compounder-cheap in consumer/utility/logistics. To refresh: re-run bank_lens_v3 (vnstock) + cash_machine_screen + unified_screener. Caveat: screening-not-advice; NEUTRAL state (not ideal crisis/bear timing); bank liqB cosmetic-0.
41	**FULL 5-LAYER STACK (2026-05-31, complete):** (1) sector ROUTER bank/cyclical/compounder; (2) valuation by correct lens; (3) ENGINE badge from engine_class.csv [COMPOUNDER=cash+asset-growth+ROIC≥12% / YIELD=cash-cow-no-runway / LOWROIC_GROWTH=value-destructive], banks excluded; (4) cash-machine◆ (CFO>NP TTM); (5) EVENT_CHECK overlay (value-trap→manual, DGC/PAT special-sit). Engine badge operationalizes the runway insight — **QTP auto-demoted from multibagger (engine=YIELD◆) despite cheap+cash-machine = dividend play not multibag** (exactly the user's QTP-vs-VCS/DGC point). Output 2026-05-31: ★GOLDEN (cheap_quality + COMPOUNDER engine, liquid) = DHA,HAH,LHG,NNC,NTP,PTB,SCS,TLG; ★★+strict-cash-machine◆ = HAH,PTB (HAH=cyclical shipping caveat). System complete: sector-aware valuation × quality-engine × cash-machine × event-overlay.
42	
43	## DNA CARD capstone + MOAT dimension (2026-05-31, `dna_card.py`) — full 8-dim per-ticker profile
44	User added MOAT/catalyst-durability insight (DRC capacity-doubled ×2 then REVERSED = no moat/thin-margin/commodity tire; VCS tech+brand global-TM = durable; DHC location moat = durable while structural edge lasted, now saturated). **Principle: capacity-catalyst × MOAT = durable multibag; catalyst WITHOUT moat = transient pop.** MOAT data-proxy = GPM level + stability + ROIC (pricing-power moats = high/stable margin+ROIC: STRONG if ROE≥18&GPM≥25, WEAK if GPM<15|ROE<10) + manual MOAT_TYPE tag (location/scale moats invisible in margins, e.g. DHC). `dna_card.py` assembles ALL dims per ticker: ROUTE·VALUATION·ENGINE·CASH◆·MOAT·MARGIN-CYCLE·RUNWAY/TAM·EVENT (reads cached unified_screener/engine_class/margin_cycle/saturation/bank_lens_v3 + moat pull). Validation: VCS STRONG[tech+brand], DRC WEAK[commodity]→reversed, DHC MODERATE[location] ✓. Standouts: **SCS** (STRONG infra-monopoly GPM78%/ROE47% + COMPOUNDER + DURABLE + cheap = best profile); QTP YIELD+WEAK-moat=dividend-not-multibag; BMP/VNM strong-moat but margin-peak/mature-runway=limited upside; **FMC weak-moat(commodity seafood GPM12%) despite cash-machine = DRC-like durability risk** (card surfaces hidden weakness). Files: dna_card.py, data/dna_cards.{md,csv}. **DONE: dna_card.py rewritten SELF-CONTAINED, 100% coverage** — computes ALL dims on-the-fly from BQ per any ticker (ICB-fallback router: 8355→BANK, COMMODITY_MAP→CYCLICAL, else→COMPOUNDER; banks read cached vnstock bank_lens_v3). GPM_P0 only in ticker_financial not ticker (gpm pulled from q-series). New examples validated: DRC[COMPOUNDER/CHEAP but Moat WEAK-commodity tire + dilut3y +30% (capacity raise) + MATURE] = card exposes "no-moat+dilutive catalyst→transient"; DHC[Moat MODERATE-LOCATION + SATURATING rev +26%→0%] = "location moat, now saturated" ✓. Standout SCS (STRONG infra-monopoly GPM78/ROE47 + COMPOUNDER + DURABLE + cheap). Drop ANY ticker → full 8-dim profile. **8 dims, each from a user insight — comprehensive cognitive framework; remaining gaps = data only (export%, P4/sulfur series, paid feeds).**
45	
46	## FUTURE RESEARCH — "what to DO with FA" (noted 2026-05-29, user insight)
47	**User's framing insight (empirically confirmed by all 5 tests today): "FA tốt chưa chắc đầu tư ngắn hạn hợp"** — good fundamentals do NOT suit the short-term momentum book. FA predicts LONG-horizon / cross-sectional-average returns, but in the capacity-bound (12-slot) short-term BA book it's a GATE (C/D + E-exclusion), not a ranker. So the question is not "evaluate companies better" (ruled out: improving the ranker doesn't move a gated capacity-bound book) but "**change HOW FA participates**". Hypotheses to explore later:
48	1. **FA as SIZING, not gating** — position size ∝ FA quality (within the momentum book) instead of C/D pass/fail. Test if quality-weighted sizing improves risk-adj returns without removing momentum winners.
49	2. **Separate / larger COMPOUNDER book** — FA-driven long-horizon hold channel (bank model gave +0.95pp HERE; COMPOUNDER is currently small & separate). Test bigger allocation + FA as the ranker in THIS book where horizon is long.
50	3. **FA edge by HORIZON** — test FA IC at 6M/1Y/2Y (ticker_1m has O6M/O1Y/O2Y). Hypothesis: FA IC RISES with horizon (today's tests were all profit_3M). If true → FA belongs in a long-horizon sleeve, formalizes the user's insight.
51	4. **Relax capacity then re-test** — does any FA improvement (EW5, bank) matter when max_pos is larger / sector cap looser? Isolates whether capacity (not FA quality) is the binding constraint.
52	5. **FA regime-conditioning** — down-weight/bypass FA gating in BULL (FA IC ≈0 in BULL per finding #2), full weight in CRISIS/NEUTRAL/EX-BULL.
53	
54	## HORIZON × STATE measurement DONE (2026-05-29, `fa_horizon_state_ic.py`, DT5G state)
55	Forward returns 3M/6M/1Y/2Y from ticker Close (LEAD), entry state = `vnindex_5state_dt5g_live`, universe ticker_prune, 10,955 FA-rating rows. **Confirms user's long-horizon + psychology thesis, quantified:**
56	- **(A) FA IC RISES with horizon**: total_score 3M +0.061 → 6M +0.070 → 1Y +0.059 → **2Y +0.113** (~2× at 2Y; EW5 same shape +0.066→+0.115). 1Y dips (recent VIC-narrow-rally overlap). Quality-axis alone weak (+0.03) — stability/cash/shareholder carry long-horizon signal.
57	- **(B) State gates it — RANKING vs ABSOLUTE split is the key**:
58	  - **CRISIS**: high IC all horizons (+0.117–0.141) BUT top-quintile ABSOLUTE negative through 1Y (3M −2.5%/win43%, 1Y −3.9%/win46%), only +7.5% at 2Y. → **"FA không cứu được" CONFIRMED** — FA only ranks who-bleeds-less, not a buy signal; needs 2Y to recover.
59	  - **BEAR**: resilience modest short (3M spread +1.1%) but HUGE long (2Y TOP +28.7% vs BOT −1.3% = **+30pp spread**). → **bear = best regime to ACCUMULATE quality for long hold.**
60	  - **BULL**: FA both ranks AND earns (TOP +5.2%/win64% at 3M, positive every horizon).
61	  - **EX-BULL**: FA is a DRAG short-term (3M spread −11%, junk flies in euphoria), quality only reasserts at 2Y (+12%). N=280 noisy.
62	- **Design principle for the long-horizon sleeve**: FA = 2Y selector, STRONGEST in BEAR (accumulate cheap quality); in CRISIS use FA as defensive ranking not entry; in EX-BULL de-emphasize FA (momentum's domain). Explains why FA failed the short-term momentum book (trades in BULL/EX-BULL where FA weak/drag at 3M). Files: `fa_horizon_state_ic.py`, `data/fa_horizon_state_ic.md`, `data/_fa_horizon_raw.csv`.
63	- **CRISIS→BEAR-stabilizing entry (user refinement, `fa_postcrisis_bear.py`) — directionally CONFIRMED at 2Y but ONE-EPISODE caveat.** DT5G has only **2** CRISIS→BEAR transitions (both 2022); FA data starts 2014 so 2008/2011 crises untestable → suggestive, NOT out-of-sample proven. POST_CRISIS_BEAR (state2, 5–90 sess since crisis, n=611): **3M top −7.5%/win24% (WORST — "stabilizing≠bottom, prices keep falling short-term")** but **2Y top +24.9%/win80% (highest win-rate) / spread +31.5pp (WIDEST quality-vs-junk, bottom −6.6%)** → this is where FA SELECTIVITY matters most (junk doesn't recover). Confirms user: needs long horizon + patience; quality bought cheap post-panic. Caveat2: BEAR_OTHER/IN_CRISIS also strong at 2Y (general depressed-state mean-reversion) — post-crisis-bear's distinct edge = highest win-rate + widest spread. Files: `data/fa_postcrisis_bear.md`.
64	
65	## Long-horizon FA sleeve PROTOTYPE (2026-05-29, `fa_longterm_sleeve.py`) — MIXED/NEGATIVE, instructive
66	Top-15 FA (total_score, tier≠E, liq≥2B), equal-wt, monthly rebal; DT5G posture machine (EX-BULL→DISTRIBUTE 40% equity hold cash; CRISIS→ACCUMULATE 100%). TC 0.25%. **Results: DRYPOWDER CAGR 8.40%/Sh0.48/DD−42.9% < FULLINVEST 10.46%/0.55/−42.9% ≈ VNI 10.32%/0.60/−43.6%.** Dry-powder UNDERperformed.
67	- **Diagnosis (`data/fa_sleeve_nav.csv`)**: DISTRIBUTE fired only 2021 + 2025 (EX-BULL rare, 58d/12y). ✅ **2021→2022 worked textbook** (raised cash pre-bear; DP −13.8% vs VNI −24.2% in 2022, DP NAV passed VNI). ❌ **COVID 2020 = worst DD −43%, posture ACCUMULATE/fully-invested** — exogenous shock, NO euphoria warning (un-timeable by any euphoria gauge). ❌ 2024-26: raised cash 2025 but VIC-led euphoria kept running → cash drag, +17.9% vs VNI +59.2%.
68	- **Two honest conclusions**: (1) dry-powder concept sound IN PRINCIPLE, validated in the one valuation-led bear (2022), but EX-BULL-alone trigger too rare + bypassed by exogenous shocks + early in extended euphoria. (2) **Root issue: long-only top-FA ≈ index** (FULLINVEST≈VNI, worse Sharpe) — the 2Y IC +0.11 edge is a LONG-SHORT cross-sectional spread concentrated in rare bear/crisis; VN 2014-26 is bull-dominated where FA-quality lags. Long-only-top can't capture it.
69	- **DO NOT deploy as built. DO NOT tune cash rule to "fix" COVID** (overfit to 2 episodes). Realistic reframes if revisited: euphoria gauge = VNINDEX PE/valuation percentile (not rare EX-BULL state); accept sleeve as DEFENSIVE/diversifying (cushions valuation-led bears, lags bull) not a CAGR winner; or pursue the long-SHORT spread (long top-FA / underweight bottom-FA) which is where the IC edge actually lives. Files: `fa_longterm_sleeve.py`, `data/fa_longterm_sleeve.md`, `data/fa_sleeve_nav.csv`, `data/_sleeve_prices.csv`.
70	
71	## Sleeve v2 — valuation timing (#1) + spread (#3), `fa_sleeve_v2.py` (2026-05-29) — CLOSES the FA-alpha hunt
72	Monthly, top-15 FA long-only / quintile long-short, VNINDEX_PE expanding percentile timing, TC 0.25%.
73	| variant | CAGR | Sharpe | MaxDD | Calmar |
74	|---|---|---|---|---|
75	| FULLINVEST | 10.19 | 0.54 | −42.1 | 0.24 |
76	| **PE_TIMED (#1)** | 7.64 | 0.53 | **−29.1** | 0.26 |
77	| LONGSHORT (#3) | **−5.75** | −0.24 | −66.3 | −0.09 |
78	| PE_LS | 3.43 | 0.28 | −30.9 | 0.11 |
79	| VNI B&H | 10.33 | 0.60 | −43.6 | 0.24 |
80	- **#1 PE-timing = validated DRAWDOWN reducer** (−42→−29 MaxDD, −13pp; 2018-COVID −3.7% vs VNI −22.1% — beat the EX-BULL state machine decisively since PE was high pre-2018) BUT costs −2.5pp CAGR (cash drag in expensive-but-rising mkt), Sharpe 0.53 still < VNI 0.60. Defensive overlay, NOT alpha. Confirms user "định giá > tâm lý" — on the RISK axis only.
81	- **#3 FA long-short = FAILS hard** (−5.75% CAGR, DD −66%): shorting low-FA junk gets run over because junk/high-beta LEADS in bull (EX-BULL "junk flies"). IC top−bottom spread is REAL but regime-concentrated (bear/crisis) & swamped by beta over the bull-heavy sample → NOT harvestable long-short in VN. REJECT.
82	- **FINAL VERDICT on FA-alpha hunt: no harvestable long-horizon FA alpha in VN.** Long-only top-FA ≈ index; long-short loses; valuation-timed lowers DD but lowers return (sub-index Sharpe). Root cause: VN 2014-26 bull/beta/momentum-led; FA edge is cross-sectional + bear/crisis-concentrated, inseparable from the beta that wins in bull. ONLY deployable artifact = PE-timing as a capital-preservation van (if goal is low-DD not beat-index). Files: `fa_sleeve_v2.py`, `data/fa_sleeve_v2.md`, `data/fa_sleeve_v2_nav.csv`.
83	
84	## Prioritized roadmap (updated — final)
85	- ❌ EW5 composite: signal-level PASS but FULL-NAV FAIL (−0.27pp net, flat Sharpe) → NOT deployed. FA is a gate, not a ranker, in a capacity-bound book.
86	- ❌ redflag exclusion in BA-core: REJECTED (removes winners). Re-target to universe pre-screen / compounder book.
87	- ❌ bank sub-model in BA-core: REJECTED (see #3). Modest win in COMPOUNDER channel only.
88	- Overlay: down-weight FA tier in BULL state (untested; lower priority).
89	- Rejected: Q×V interaction; net_issuance & accruals as rankers.
90

file.content:
---
name: fa-layer-ic-audit-2026
description: "FA 7-axis layer audit — 2 axes have negative IC, bank gap confirmed, redflag_cnt is a valid blow-up screen, value doesn't work in VN"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 23c2ffe0-ced2-4d30-bcff-e0b6aa1c8435
---

# FA layer (fundamental_rating.py) research audit — 2026-05-29

Empirical audit of the 7-axis FA layer that feeds v11/v12 stock selection (`fundamental_rating.py` → tier A/B/C/D/E → `recommend_holistic.py`). Methodology: Spearman IC vs forward `profit_3M` on `fundamental_rating_all.csv` (11,206 rows w/ outcome, 2014-2026), IS 2014-19 / OOS 2020+ split, regime-conditional via `vnindex_5state`, tail-risk for screens. Scripts: `fa_ic_audit.py`, `fa_ic_composites.py`, `fa_newfactor_ic.py`, `fa_sector_and_value.py`. Reports: `data/fa_ic_audit.md`, `fa_newfactor_ic.md`, `fa_sector_value.md`.

**How FA is USED (key framing):** FA tier is NOT the alpha selector in the BA book — MEGA/MOMENTUM entries require fa_tier∈{C,D}+momentum+BULL; A/B route to COMPOUNDER/WAIT. FA = (a) exclusion floor (tier E=AVOID) + (b) compounder router. So "evaluate companies better" = better blow-up avoidance + better compounder ID, two different jobs.

## Findings (4 directions, all empirically tested)

1. **2 axes are dead weight — DROP them (highest-ROI, lowest-risk).** `health` (IC −0.04 ALL, −0.09 IS) and `valuation` (−0.03 ALL, −0.10 IS) have NEGATIVE IC in BOTH IS and OOS. 5-axis composite **EW5** = quality+stability+cash+shareholder+growth beats 7-axis on IC (OOS 0.089 vs 0.088, ALL 0.102 vs 0.095) AND decile spread (+6.83 vs +6.53pp OOS), both periods. CORE4 (also drop growth) best OOS IC 0.093 but smaller spread. Original designer down-weighted these (8%/10%, smallest) — data says go further, drop. **MUST validate via integrated V11/V12 backtest before deploy** (codebase rule: never trust standalone IC).

2. **FA edge is regime-concentrated, ZERO in BULL.** total_score IC: CRISIS +0.155, EX-BULL +0.169, NEUTRAL +0.094, BEAR +0.037, **BULL −0.003**. Confirms [[feedback-bull-market-psychology]] at stock level — fundamentals don't predict in BULL (FOMO rules). → consider down-weighting/bypassing FA tier gating in BULL. EX-BULL: high IC but ALL tiers negative (−6 to −11%) = "sell-everything" regime, FA still ranks (best falls least).

3. **Bank gap CONFIRMED + PROTOTYPE BUILT (2026-05-29).** Sector-8 IC +0.061 vs non-fin +0.124 (HALF). **Decompose by sub-code**: sector 8 = Banks 8355, RealEstate 8633 (104 tickers, lumpy), FinServices 8777, Insurance 8536 — NOT all banks. True banks (8355, 27 tickers, 751 rows w/ outcome): generic IC OOS −0.045 (actively misleads modern era!), quality-axis ≈0. **`fa_bank_submodel.py` prototype** — 4 bank axes (Profit 35% ROE_Trailing+ROA, Growth 25% NP_R+asset-growth+RevYoY, Safety 25% OwnEq_Cap[CAR proxy]+ROE_Min5Y+−NP_CV, Value 15% −PB_self_z+ROE/PB), percentile within-bank cohort/quarter. **Result: beats generic head-to-head OOS +0.047 vs −0.045 (+0.092 swing)**; OOS tercile CLEAN monotonic (Top +4.68%/win59.7% > Mid +3.11% > Bot +1.03%/win52.7%); 5-tier too granular for 27 names (use tercile). BQ lacks NIM/NPL/CASA/CAR; proxies work. **Data caveat: latest-quarter BS ratios (OwnEq_Cap, FinLev) arrive as 0 → forward-fill last positive per ticker** (coverage check counts 0 as non-null, misleading). Files: `fa_bank_submodel.py`, `fundamental_rating_banks.csv`, `data/fa_bank_submodel.md`. RealEstate 8633 (bigger, IC +0.049) is a separate future sub-model.

   **INTEGRATED VALIDATION DONE (2026-05-29) — textbook IC-vs-integrated reversal.** Swapped bank_tier→generic for ICB-8355 in v11 BA-core classify (`fa_bank_integrated.py`, ports recommend_holistic.classify_play_type, 603k prune rows, realized profit_3M). **Standalone IC said bank model WINS (+0.047 vs −0.045 OOS) but integrated REJECTS it for BA-core**: full book annualized 70.0%→66.9% (−3.1pp), bank-only OOS 93.6%→87.1%. Smoking gun: bank model ADDS losers (482 sig, mean −2.18%/win29%) & REMOVES winners (704 sig, +10.44%/win63.6%). **WHY: BA-core requires fa_tier∈{C,D} — FA used INVERTED (momentum alpha on non-pristine names); a better quality ranker pushes high-ROE banks (VCB/MBB) to A/B → kicks momentum-breakout winners OUT.** Improving company-quality is COUNTERPRODUCTIVE for the momentum book. **Right channel = COMPOUNDER_BUY (A/B accumulate): bank model HELPS +0.95pp mean (7.00→7.95%) / +1.2pp win** — modest, correctly-signed. Quality-hold broad route flat (−0.06pp); AVOID_faE exclusion identical (proxies miss NPL/asset-quality, bank crashes rare). **VERDICT: do NOT feed bank_tier into BA-core; narrow modest benefit only in COMPOUNDER book (separate small allocation). Keep FA as pure E-exclusion floor for banks in the momentum book.** Confirms the framing: FA in BA book = exclusion+router, NOT quality selector — proves "never trust standalone IC, must integrated-test". Files: `fa_bank_integrated.py`, `data/fa_bank_integrated.md`, `data/_fa_bank_integrated_raw.csv`.

4. **redflag_cnt = validated blow-up SCREEN (2nd concrete win).** Beneish-lite count {DSO surge>1.3, inventory surge>1.3, GPM decline, leverage up>1.1×}. IC only −0.04 BUT monotonic crash gradient: 0 flags→crash 11.8%/p5 −30%; 4 flags→18.4%/p5 −43%/median −4.24%. Evaluate exclusion screens by TAIL not IC (per [[feedback-us-vn-correlation-interpretation]]). → add as penalty/cap (flags≥3), NOT a ranker. By contrast net_issuance (sign-flips IS+0.04/OOS−0.05) & accruals (non-monotonic tail) FAIL; combining all 3 naively HURTS EW5 (OOS 0.072 vs 0.086).

5. **Quality×Value interaction — NEGATIVE result, DON'T build.** Cheap doesn't win even gated by quality (valuation IC negative in all 3 quality terciles: LowQ −0.035→HighQ −0.014). 2×2 median profit_3M: HighQ+Expensive 0.00%, HighQ+Cheap −0.08%, LowQ+Expensive −1.64%, **LowQ+Cheap −2.79% (value trap)**. Quality dominates; cheapness is mostly a trap in VN (momentum/growth frontier market). Keep valuation out of positive composite (reinforces #1); optionally retain only extreme-overvaluation cap (BA already has via PE z-score).

## Quick-win integrated results (2026-05-29, `fa_quickwins_integrated.py`, same v11 BA-core harness)
- **EW5 (drop health+valuation) — signal-level PASS but FULL-NAV FAIL ❌ (do NOT deploy).** Signal-level (equal-weight, unlimited capital): full BA-core annualized 70.0%→72.7% (+2.7pp), OOS +3.0pp, composition favorable. **BUT full prod-spec NAV (`run_5systems_prodspec.py`, fa_ratings_ew5 table + ba_v11_ew5_sig.pkl, max_pos=12/cap/TC) REVERSES it**: V1 16.13→15.65 (−0.48), V4 20.58→19.75 (−0.83), V5 21.99→21.32 (−0.67); only V2/V3 +0.31 (noise); Sharpe flat ±0.02; DD mixed. Net ≈−0.27pp. **WHY: FA tier is a GATE (C/D) + E-exclusion, NOT the ranker — `ta` ranks the 12 slots; capacity-bound book realizes ~same top-ta names regardless of FA reshuffle.** Signal-level counted all ~10k signals; portfolio holds 12. Files: `data/fa_ew5_fullnav_compare.md`, `data/5sys_prodspec_*_{base,ew5}.csv`, `build_ew5_pkl.py`, BQ `tav2_bq.fa_ratings_ew5`. Baseline reproduced canonical (V1 16.13≈16.97, V5 21.99≈22.08).
- **redflag≥3 exclusion in BA-core — FAILS ❌.** Annualized 70.0%→69.3% (−0.7pp), OOS −0.7pp. Smoking gun: removes 635 sig that are BETTER than kept (mean +16.03% vs +14.07%, crash 6.3% vs 8.5%). In the momentum book, flagged-but-high-momentum-in-BULL names are still good 3M trades (FOMO dominates short horizon; red flags are slow-burn). **redflag value is UNIVERSE-level junk screen / COMPOUNDER book ONLY (validated tail gradient earlier), NOT a momentum-book exclusion.**

**Meta-lesson (consistent across bank model + redflag + inverted-FA):** the BA-core MOMENTUM book RESISTS fundamental overlays — its edge is technical. FA-quality/risk screens that help average/long-horizon selection actively HURT the momentum book by removing high-momentum names with imperfect fundamentals. Only EW5 helps (removes noise, doesn't add a constraint).

## Bank model — REAL ratios via vnstock + asset-quality GATE (2026-05-31)
BQ ticker_financial (170 cols) has NO bank fields (no NPL/CAR/NIM/CASA — verified by full scan); generic metrics (GPM/inventory/DSO) misfire for banks. **vnstock (VCI) `Vnstock().stock(sym,source='VCI').finance.ratio(period='quarter')` HAS real bank ratios**: NPL, coverage (LLR/NPL), CAR, NIM, CIR, CASA, loan/deposit growth, 40q history. Quirks: dedupe cols (dup "Cost to Income Ratio"); coverage/CIR stored negative→abs; CAR only in annual rows (lengthReport==5); pace requests + retry (VCI DNS flaky, rapid loop gets reset). Bank lens v3 (`bank_lens_v3.py`): asset-quality GATE (AVOID NPL>3%|cov<50%|CAR<9%|ROE<8% ; WATCH NPL>2%|cov<80%|NPL rising>+0.5pp/4q ; else CLEAN) + NPL TREND (rising worse than level) → rank CLEAN by quality 0.6 + value 0.4 (P/B-vs-ROE). **Result (2026-05-31, 18 banks): CLEAN=VCB/BID/CTG/TCB/MBB/ACB; deployable shortlist TCB/ACB/MBB/CTG (clean+ROE 15-22%+fair PB); VCB pristine(NPL0.62/cov253) but PB2.21 expensive. STB=AVOID NPL 6.62% +4.11pp/yr EXPLODING. Most banks' NPL FALLING (de-stressing from 2023).** Nuance: AVOID banks w/ NPL falling fast (VPB −1.16, VIB −0.85) = turnaround-watch bucket (cyclical-trough logic). MBB +9x since 2017 validated: bought PB 0.91 + ROE 14→23% compounding. Files: bank_lens_v3.py, bank_valuation_lens.py, fa_bank_submodel.py, data/bank_lens_v3.{md,csv}. **3 separate frameworks now: QT v4 (compounder, idea-gen) / cyclical-commodity (commodity trough) / bank (NPL-gate + PB-ROE).**

## UNIFIED SCREENER (2026-05-31, `unified_screener.py`) — capstone: auto-routes each ticker to its framework
Router: ICB 8355 → BANK (cached bank_lens_v3.csv); COMMODITY_MAP (rubber/iron_ore/urea/dap tickers) → CYCLICAL (commodity-pctile regime + stock dd dislocation); else → COMPOUNDER (multi-lens cheap: PEG/pe_z/pb_z + value-trap guard). Normalized actions: BUY-zone / BUY-eligible / ACCUMULATE / WATCH / WAIT / AVOID-new / AVOID. Each ticker scored ONLY by its sector-correct lens (banks not by PEG, cyclicals not by PE-level, etc.). Output data/unified_screener.{md,csv}. **2026-05-29 result — BUY-zone**: COMPOUNDER {VNM,SCS,QTP,BWE,TLG,PTB,PVT,NNC,DHA,HAH}; CYCLICAL {NKG,SMC,POM=steel TROUGH_BUY (iron-ore pctile0.35 + dd<−30%); HPG/HSG=ACCUMULATE; rubber/urea=AVOID-new late-cycle; DAP=WAIT}; BANK {ACB,CTG,MBB=CLEAN+cheap; STB=AVOID NPL exploding}. Cross-framework read: steel=cyclical buy-zone, banks clean, compounder-cheap in consumer/utility/logistics. To refresh: re-run bank_lens_v3 (vnstock) + cash_machine_screen + unified_screener. Caveat: screening-not-advice; NEUTRAL state (not ideal crisis/bear timing); bank liqB cosmetic-0.
**FULL 5-LAYER STACK (2026-05-31, complete):** (1) sector ROUTER bank/cyclical/compounder; (2) valuation by correct lens; (3) ENGINE badge from engine_class.csv [COMPOUNDER=cash+asset-growth+ROIC≥12% / YIELD=cash-cow-no-runway / LOWROIC_GROWTH=value-destructive], banks excluded; (4) cash-machine◆ (CFO>NP TTM); (5) EVENT_CHECK overlay (value-trap→manual, DGC/PAT special-sit). Engine badge operationalizes the runway insight — **QTP auto-demoted from multibagger (engine=YIELD◆) despite cheap+cash-machine = dividend play not multibag** (exactly the user's QTP-vs-VCS/DGC point). Output 2026-05-31: ★GOLDEN (cheap_quality + COMPOUNDER engine, liquid) = DHA,HAH,LHG,NNC,NTP,PTB,SCS,TLG; ★★+strict-cash-machine◆ = HAH,PTB (HAH=cyclical shipping caveat). System complete: sector-aware valuation × quality-engine × cash-machine × event-overlay.

## DNA CARD capstone + MOAT dimension (2026-05-31, `dna_card.py`) — full 8-dim per-ticker profile
User added MOAT/catalyst-durability insight (DRC capacity-doubled ×2 then REVERSED = no moat/thin-margin/commodity tire; VCS tech+brand global-TM = durable; DHC location moat = durable while structural edge lasted, now saturated). **Principle: capacity-catalyst × MOAT = durable multibag; catalyst WITHOUT moat = transient pop.** MOAT data-proxy = GPM level + stability + ROIC (pricing-power moats = high/stable margin+ROIC: STRONG if ROE≥18&GPM≥25, WEAK if GPM<15|ROE<10) + manual MOAT_TYPE tag (location/scale moats invisible in margins, e.g. DHC). `dna_card.py` assembles ALL dims per ticker: ROUTE·VALUATION·ENGINE·CASH◆·MOAT·MARGIN-CYCLE·RUNWAY/TAM·EVENT (reads cached unified_screener/engine_class/margin_cycle/saturation/bank_lens_v3 + moat pull). Validation: VCS STRONG[tech+brand], DRC WEAK[commodity]→reversed, DHC MODERATE[location] ✓. Standouts: **SCS** (STRONG infra-monopoly GPM78%/ROE47% + COMPOUNDER + DURABLE + cheap = best profile); QTP YIELD+WEAK-moat=dividend-not-multibag; BMP/VNM strong-moat but margin-peak/mature-runway=limited upside; **FMC weak-moat(commodity seafood GPM12%) despite cash-machine = DRC-like durability risk** (card surfaces hidden weakness). Files: dna_card.py, data/dna_cards.{md,csv}. **DONE: dna_card.py rewritten SELF-CONTAINED, 100% coverage** — computes ALL dims on-the-fly from BQ per any ticker (ICB-fallback router: 8355→BANK, COMMODITY_MAP→CYCLICAL, else→COMPOUNDER; banks read cached vnstock bank_lens_v3). GPM_P0 only in ticker_financial not ticker (gpm pulled from q-series). New examples validated: DRC[COMPOUNDER/CHEAP but Moat WEAK-commodity tire + dilut3y +30% (capacity raise) + MATURE] = card exposes "no-moat+dilutive catalyst→transient"; DHC[Moat MODERATE-LOCATION + SATURATING rev +26%→0%] = "location moat, now saturated" ✓. Standout SCS (STRONG infra-monopoly GPM78/ROE47 + COMPOUNDER + DURABLE + cheap). Drop ANY ticker → full 8-dim profile. **8 dims, each from a user insight — comprehensive cognitive framework; remaining gaps = data only (export%, P4/sulfur series, paid feeds).**

## FUTURE RESEARCH — "what to DO with FA" (noted 2026-05-29, user insight)
**User's framing insight (empirically confirmed by all 5 tests today): "FA tốt chưa chắc đầu tư ngắn hạn hợp"** — good fundamentals do NOT suit the short-term momentum book. FA predicts LONG-horizon / cross-sectional-average returns, but in the capacity-bound (12-slot) short-term BA book it's a GATE (C/D + E-exclusion), not a ranker. So the question is not "evaluate companies better" (ruled out: improving the ranker doesn't move a gated capacity-bound book) but "**change HOW FA participates**". Hypotheses to explore later:
1. **FA as SIZING, not gating** — position size ∝ FA quality (within the momentum book) instead of C/D pass/fail. Test if quality-weighted sizing improves risk-adj returns without removing momentum winners.
2. **Separate / larger COMPOUNDER book** — FA-driven long-horizon hold channel (bank model gave +0.95pp HERE; COMPOUNDER is currently small & separate). Test bigger allocation + FA as the ranker in THIS book where horizon is long.
3. **FA edge by HORIZON** — test FA IC at 6M/1Y/2Y (ticker_1m has O6M/O1Y/O2Y). Hypothesis: FA IC RISES with horizon (today's tests were all profit_3M). If true → FA belongs in a long-horizon sleeve, formalizes the user's insight.
4. **Relax capacity then re-test** — does any FA improvement (EW5, bank) matter when max_pos is larger / sector cap looser? Isolates whether capacity (not FA quality) is the binding constraint.
5. **FA regime-conditioning** — down-weight/bypass FA gating in BULL (FA IC ≈0 in BULL per finding #2), full weight in CRISIS/NEUTRAL/EX-BULL.

## HORIZON × STATE measurement DONE (2026-05-29, `fa_horizon_state_ic.py`, DT5G state)
Forward returns 3M/6M/1Y/2Y from ticker Close (LEAD), entry state = `vnindex_5state_dt5g_live`, universe ticker_prune, 10,955 FA-rating rows. **Confirms user's long-horizon + psychology thesis, quantified:**
- **(A) FA IC RISES with horizon**: total_score 3M +0.061 → 6M +0.070 → 1Y +0.059 → **2Y +0.113** (~2× at 2Y; EW5 same shape +0.066→+0.115). 1Y dips (recent VIC-narrow-rally overlap). Quality-axis alone weak (+0.03) — stability/cash/shareholder carry long-horizon signal.
- **(B) State gates it — RANKING vs ABSOLUTE split is the key**:
  - **CRISIS**: high IC all horizons (+0.117–0.141) BUT top-quintile ABSOLUTE negative through 1Y (3M −2.5%/win43%, 1Y −3.9%/win46%), only +7.5% at 2Y. → **"FA không cứu được" CONFIRMED** — FA only ranks who-bleeds-less, not a buy signal; needs 2Y to recover.
  - **BEAR**: resilience modest short (3M spread +1.1%) but HUGE long (2Y TOP +28.7% vs BOT −1.3% = **+30pp spread**). → **bear = best regime to ACCUMULATE quality for long hold.**
  - **BULL**: FA both ranks AND earns (TOP +5.2%/win64% at 3M, positive every horizon).
  - **EX-BULL**: FA is a DRAG short-term (3M spread −11%, junk flies in euphoria), quality only reasserts at 2Y (+12%). N=280 noisy.
- **Design principle for the long-horizon sleeve**: FA = 2Y selector, STRONGEST in BEAR (accumulate cheap quality); in CRISIS use FA as defensive ranking not entry; in EX-BULL de-emphasize FA (momentum's domain). Explains why FA failed the short-term momentum book (trades in BULL/EX-BULL where FA weak/drag at 3M). Files: `fa_horizon_state_ic.py`, `data/fa_horizon_state_ic.md`, `data/_fa_horizon_raw.csv`.
- **CRISIS→BEAR-stabilizing entry (user refinement, `fa_postcrisis_bear.py`) — directionally CONFIRMED at 2Y but ONE-EPISODE caveat.** DT5G has only **2** CRISIS→BEAR transitions (both 2022); FA data starts 2014 so 2008/2011 crises untestable → suggestive, NOT out-of-sample proven. POST_CRISIS_BEAR (state2, 5–90 sess since crisis, n=611): **3M top −7.5%/win24% (WORST — "stabilizing≠bottom, prices keep falling short-term")** but **2Y top +24.9%/win80% (highest win-rate) / spread +31.5pp (WIDEST quality-vs-junk, bottom −6.6%)** → this is where FA SELECTIVITY matters most (junk doesn't recover). Confirms user: needs long horizon + patience; quality bought cheap post-panic. Caveat2: BEAR_OTHER/IN_CRISIS also strong at 2Y (general depressed-state mean-reversion) — post-crisis-bear's distinct edge = highest win-rate + widest spread. Files: `data/fa_postcrisis_bear.md`.

## Long-horizon FA sleeve PROTOTYPE (2026-05-29, `fa_longterm_sleeve.py`) — MIXED/NEGATIVE, instructive
Top-15 FA (total_score, tier≠E, liq≥2B), equal-wt, monthly rebal; DT5G posture machine (EX-BULL→DISTRIBUTE 40% equity hold cash; CRISIS→ACCUMULATE 100%). TC 0.25%. **Results: DRYPOWDER CAGR 8.40%/Sh0.48/DD−42.9% < FULLINVEST 10.46%/0.55/−42.9% ≈ VNI 10.32%/0.60/−43.6%.** Dry-powder UNDERperformed.
- **Diagnosis (`data/fa_sleeve_nav.csv`)**: DISTRIBUTE fired only 2021 + 2025 (EX-BULL rare, 58d/12y). ✅ **2021→2022 worked textbook** (raised cash pre-bear; DP −13.8% vs VNI −24.2% in 2022, DP NAV passed VNI). ❌ **COVID 2020 = worst DD −43%, posture ACCUMULATE/fully-invested** — exogenous shock, NO euphoria warning (un-timeable by any euphoria gauge). ❌ 2024-26: raised cash 2025 but VIC-led euphoria kept running → cash drag, +17.9% vs VNI +59.2%.
- **Two honest conclusions**: (1) dry-powder concept sound IN PRINCIPLE, validated in the one valuation-led bear (2022), but EX-BULL-alone trigger too rare + bypassed by exogenous shocks + early in extended euphoria. (2) **Root issue: long-only top-FA ≈ index** (FULLINVEST≈VNI, worse Sharpe) — the 2Y IC +0.11 edge is a LONG-SHORT cross-sectional spread concentrated in rare bear/crisis; VN 2014-26 is bull-dominated where FA-quality lags. Long-only-top can't capture it.
- **DO NOT deploy as built. DO NOT tune cash rule to "fix" COVID** (overfit to 2 episodes). Realistic reframes if revisited: euphoria gauge = VNINDEX PE/valuation percentile (not rare EX-BULL state); accept sleeve as DEFENSIVE/diversifying (cushions valuation-led bears, lags bull) not a CAGR winner; or pursue the long-SHORT spread (long top-FA / underweight bottom-FA) which is where the IC edge actually lives. Files: `fa_longterm_sleeve.py`, `data/fa_longterm_sleeve.md`, `data/fa_sleeve_nav.csv`, `data/_sleeve_prices.csv`.

## Sleeve v2 — valuation timing (#1) + spread (#3), `fa_sleeve_v2.py` (2026-05-29) — CLOSES the FA-alpha hunt
Monthly, top-15 FA long-only / quintile long-short, VNINDEX_PE expanding percentile timing, TC 0.25%.
| variant | CAGR | Sharpe | MaxDD | Calmar |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FULLINVEST | 10.19 | 0.54 | −42.1 | 0.24 |
| **PE_TIMED (#1)** | 7.64 | 0.53 | **−29.1** | 0.26 |
| LONGSHORT (#3) | **−5.75** | −0.24 | −66.3 | −0.09 |
| PE_LS | 3.43 | 0.28 | −30.9 | 0.11 |
| VNI B&H | 10.33 | 0.60 | −43.6 | 0.24 |
- **#1 PE-timing = validated DRAWDOWN reducer** (−42→−29 MaxDD, −13pp; 2018-COVID −3.7% vs VNI −22.1% — beat the EX-BULL state machine decisively since PE was high pre-2018) BUT costs −2.5pp CAGR (cash drag in expensive-but-rising mkt), Sharpe 0.53 still < VNI 0.60. Defensive overlay, NOT alpha. Confirms user "định giá > tâm lý" — on the RISK axis only.
- **#3 FA long-short = FAILS hard** (−5.75% CAGR, DD −66%): shorting low-FA junk gets run over because junk/high-beta LEADS in bull (EX-BULL "junk flies"). IC top−bottom spread is REAL but regime-concentrated (bear/crisis) & swamped by beta over the bull-heavy sample → NOT harvestable long-short in VN. REJECT.
- **FINAL VERDICT on FA-alpha hunt: no harvestable long-horizon FA alpha in VN.** Long-only top-FA ≈ index; long-short loses; valuation-timed lowers DD but lowers return (sub-index Sharpe). Root cause: VN 2014-26 bull/beta/momentum-led; FA edge is cross-sectional + bear/crisis-concentrated, inseparable from the beta that wins in bull. ONLY deployable artifact = PE-timing as a capital-preservation van (if goal is low-DD not beat-index). Files: `fa_sleeve_v2.py`, `data/fa_sleeve_v2.md`, `data/fa_sleeve_v2_nav.csv`.

## Prioritized roadmap (updated — final)
- ❌ EW5 composite: signal-level PASS but FULL-NAV FAIL (−0.27pp net, flat Sharpe) → NOT deployed. FA is a gate, not a ranker, in a capacity-bound book.
- ❌ redflag exclusion in BA-core: REJECTED (removes winners). Re-target to universe pre-screen / compounder book.
- ❌ bank sub-model in BA-core: REJECTED (see #3). Modest win in COMPOUNDER channel only.
- Overlay: down-weight FA tier in BULL state (untested; lower priority).
- Rejected: Q×V interaction; net_issuance & accruals as rankers.

58	**BACKTEST 2-trục ([REDACTED]02, (c) đã làm)**: ticker 2014+, fwd-12M LEAD252. Pooled med12M/win/p25: baseline 5.9/57/−14.2 · QUAL-only(ROIC3Y≥.10&ROE_Min3Y≥.05) 9.5/63/−8.6 · DIS-only(pbz≤−1) 14.3/65/−7.6 · **DIS-TRAP(ROE_Min5Y<0) 8.8/58/−12.5** · DIS+book-guard 15.9/67/−5.6 · **COMBINED(QUAL+DIS+book) 18.6/70.5/−3.7**. KẾT LUẬN: (1) kết hợp 2-trục THẮNG từng trục riêng mọi chiều (return+win+downside); (2) **book-guard validated bằng số liệu** — capital-destroyer dislocation chỉ 8.8% (≈baseline) vs guarded 15.9% → bẫy HAG thật, kéo tụt tín hiệu; (3) downside đơn điệu tốt dần. **By-year: contrarian** — thắng mạnh năm gấu/hồi phục (2022 +8.2 vs mkt−10.3, 2023 +17 vs 8) NHƯNG thua bull momentum cực mạnh (2017 −41 vs +2 n=210, lag 2020 V-recovery). Drawdown-filter KHÔNG cải thiện sạch (mixed) → không thêm. Bản chất = tín hiệu "mua sợ hãi", lag melt-up euphoric = đánh đổi chấp nhận được (đúng triết lý). Deploy-worthy; nên pair Ngũ Hành để né deploy trong bull cực mạnh.
59	
60	**🔑 DT5G TABLE CONVENTION (user-confirmed [REDACTED]02: MỌI quyết định dùng DT5G làm nền)**: DT5G = **`tav2_bq.vnindex_5state_dt5g_live`** (published bởi deploy_golive_dt5g_v4/publish_gated_state.py LAYER1, có macro-healthcheck fail-safe→DT4; đọc bởi golive_recommend.py DT_TABLE, crisis_release, fa_postcrisis_bear, fa_longterm_sleeve, fa_horizon_state_ic). **CANONICAL `vnindex_5state` = LEGACY (Tinh Tế/v2g_pe3c/v3.4b lineage), KHÁC DT5G 35% lịch sử — CHỈ research/backtest/compare cũ đọc, KHÔNG phải đường live.** RULE: mọi consumer LIVE/mới phải đọc `_dt5g_live`, KHÔNG đọc canonical mộc. **FIXED: cheap_pb_floor.py Ngũ Hành-gate đổi `vnindex_5state`→`vnindex_5state_dt5g_live`** (đã align). States 1-5 = CRISIS/BEAR/NEUTRAL/BULL/EX-BULL; hiện NEUTRAL(3). **SYNC DONE [REDACTED]02 (user approved)**: canonical `vnindex_5state` 2014+ giờ = DT5G **100% match** (pre-2014 GIỮ từ archive, không mất data; range 2000→2026 6290 rows). Bản cũ → `vnindex_5state_archive_pre_dt5g_20260602`. **⚠️ ONE-TIME — sync sẽ bị REVERT nếu deploy_v2g_pe3c_s3.py/deploy_ngu_hanh.py (ghi series cũ vào canonical) chạy lại.** Để bền: hoặc cho publish_gated_state.py mirror DT5G→canonical mỗi ngày (file trong package deploy_golive_dt5g_v4 ship cho dev, cần approve sửa), hoặc ngừng các deploy series-cũ. cheap_pb_floor đọc dt5g_live trực tiếp nên không phụ thuộc canonical.
61	
62	**DAILY REPORT EDITS ([REDACTED]02, user)**: telegram_recommend.py (18:00 report) sửa: (1) header "BA-SYSTEM v11 LIVE"→**"🛰️ V4 12.1 (DT5G) REPORT"**; (2) strategy line→V4 12.1 (BAL+Ensemble VN30/LAGGED+BASE parking); (3) **thêm cột R (8L rating 1-5)** vào book table (RATING_MAP đọc rating_8l.csv); (4) **BỎ F-system overlay** (cả 2 nhánh); (5) cash-msg "BA-system"→"V4 12.1"; (6) section ③ MARKET-STATE bỏ dòng "State:" (đã có ở header regime), giữ As-of+Source; (7) dòng NAV V4 12.1 ghi rõ "**chạy từ {start}**"; Book B đổi nhãn "Ensemble leg VN30/LAGGED". **8L task `8L_Daily_Alert` đổi 15:30→17:45** (qua PowerShell Set-ScheduledTask; schtasks /change treo vì hỏi password) — chạy trước report 18:00 để rating fresh. **✅ GOLIVE WIRED ([REDACTED]02): report giờ hiển thị PICKS V4 12.1 THẬT.** (1) golive_recommend.py ghi cả LAGGED entries vào CSV (trước chỉ MD); (2) papertrade_daily.bat thêm bước [0e] golive_recommend (sinh out/golive_recommendations_<date>.csv lúc 15:30 sau publish DT5G); (3) telegram_recommend.main() đọc golive CSV của ngày → lấy ticker list (BAL + leg active VN30/LAGGED), hiển thị bằng df_liq (Close/score/FA/RSI/**R rating**); Book B nhãn động theo mode; FALLBACK BA-core nếu CSV thiếu (report không vỡ). Kiến trúc: golive=picks, report=display+rating. Verified dry-run [REDACTED]02: BAL=KSF(R2), Book B trống (no 2nd-leg today).
63	
64	**(A) SLEEVE vs v11/v12.1 — VALIDATED signal-level ([REDACTED]02, analyze_rating_sleeve.py)**: rating×PB-floor sleeve = basket EW tháng của COMBINED (ROIC3Y≥.10&ROE_Min3Y≥.05 & pbz≤−1 & ROE_Min5Y≥0), ~16 mã khi active, 141/150 tháng có tín hiệu. **Corr với v11 −0.09, v12.1 −0.04, VNI −0.11** (uncorrelated; v11-v12.1 +0.93). Standalone 16.5%/DD−30.6%/Calmar0.54. **BLEND**: v12.1+20%sleeve = CAGR 20.1%(≈)/MaxDD −17→**−14.9%**/Calmar 1.19→**1.35**/Sharpe→**1.40**; v11+20% Calmar 0.90→1.02. Sweet spot 15-20%. **Smoking gun: 2022 sleeve +20.4% vs v11 +1.5% (VNI −32.8%); 2024 +21.5% vs ~+10%**. KHÁC FA-overlay fail: luồng return RIÊNG uncorrelated (cộng), không overlay sửa pick (trừ winner). **TODO: prod-spec validation (TC/liq/capacity) trước deploy sleeve.**
65	
66	**⏰ TODO THỨ BẢY (~[REDACTED]06/07) — user nhờ nhắc chạy (B)&(C)**: (B) distress-exclusion = chỉ loại rating-5 khỏi pick v11/v12.1, test cắt tail-risk không giết winner. (C) regime-conditional sizing = rating điều tiết size CHỈ trong BEAR/CRISIS (Ngũ Hành≤2). Cả 2 backtest prod-spec.
67	
68	**DEPLOYED (a)+(b) [REDACTED]02**: (a) unified_screener.py merge cột `rating` từ rating_8l.csv (hiện cột R trong .md/.csv); cheap_pb_floor.py REFACTOR = đọc rating_8l.csv, gate **rating≤3 + book-guard(ROE_Min5Y≥0) + PB_z**, tier GOLDEN(R1+z≤−1)/STRONG(R2)/SPEC(R3)/ACCUM(R1-2+z−1..−0.3)/TRAP(excl). (b) **Ngũ Hành gating**: đọc tav2_bq.vnindex_5state (1=CRISIS..5=EX-BULL; hiện NEUTRAL=3); state≥4 euphoric→cảnh báo "contrarian lags cf 2017, size nhỏ"; state≤2 stress→"cửa sổ mua tốt nhất". Telegram alert NEW vào GOLDEN/STRONG (telegram OK). **pt_8l_daily.bat 5 bước: rating_8l→unified_screener→rank_8l→rank_8l_daily_alert→cheap_pb_floor** (rating_8l PHẢI chạy đầu). Live: GOLDEN VGC/SCS/ACV, STRONG DGC/BWE, TRAP loại HAG/NRC. Pipeline: ...→rating_8l→unified_screener→rank_8l→dna_card. x-ref [[fa_layer_ic_audit_2026]], [[cheap_pb_floor_quality_crisis_2026]].
